title: psacaliopsis paneroi
intro: Psacaliopsis paneroi, also known as the Mexican devil's claw, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as harpagoside. The health benefits of Psacaliopsis paneroi include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as potential applications in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Psacaliopsis paneroi can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine in Mexico and other parts of Central America.
